name: Schizophrenia DARPin NMDA receptor modulator protein

phase_0.domain_name: Schizophrenia DARPin NMDA receptor modulator protein
phase_0.boundary_condition: the NMDA receptor's ligand-binding cleft, which contains and closes the system by defining the only site where the DARPin can bind and exert its modulatory effect

VINIT.justification: the initial, unbound state of the therapeutic protein before any interaction with the NMDA receptor — void of target engagement

TANCH.justification: the closed boundary that contains the entire system: the binding pocket defines the spatial and chemical limits of the interaction

AFWD.chosen_element: DARPin binding to the glycine site
AFWD.justification: the forward morphism — the directed transformation from unbound to bound state, representing the primary therapeutic action

AREV.chosen_element: DARPin dissociation from the glycine site
AREV.justification: the reverse/descent transformation — unbinding, which returns the system to the unbound state, representing drug clearance or off-target release

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential binding of DARPin's ankyrin repeats
CLINK.justification: composition — the stepwise engagement of individual ankyrin repeat domains with the receptor surface, chaining multiple binding events into a stable interaction

IMSCRIB.justification: identity/self-reference — the DARPin's tertiary structure is the stable, self-recognizing entity that persists through binding and unbinding cycles

FSPLIT.justification: the split — the NMDA receptor's glycine site can exist in either an open (active) or closed (inactive) conformation; the DARPin's binding selects one path

FFUSE.chosen_element: stabilization of the receptor's open conformation
FFUSE.justification: the fuse — the DARPin's binding reconstitutes the receptor into a single, stabilized open state, which is the original therapeutic target (the split input)

EVALT.justification: affirmative state — the DARPin binds and the NMDA receptor's open probability increases, achieving the desired therapeutic effect

EVALF.justification: negative state — the DARPin fails to bind, or the receptor enters a desensitized state that prevents potentiation

ENGAGR.chosen_element: partial agonism at the glycine site
ENGAGR.justification: a paradice — the DARPin simultaneously occupies the glycine site and permits partial receptor activation, a state where both binding and non-binding effects are held without full resolution

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record — the primary structure of the DARPin is fixed by its genetic code and cannot be altered during the therapeutic cycle

phase_2.split_input: the NMDA receptor's glycine site in an unbound, dynamic equilibrium between open and closed conformations
phase_2.fuse_element: stabilization of the receptor's open conformation
phase_2.fuse_result: the NMDA receptor's glycine site in a stabilized open conformation — semantically equal to the split input's target state

phase_3.void_description: the DARPin in solution, unbound and not yet interacting with any receptor — 00, uninitialized
phase_3.true_description: the DARPin bound to the glycine site, receptor potentiated — 01, affirmative, success
phase_3.false_description: the DARPin dissociated or the receptor desensitized — 10, negative, failure
phase_3.both_description: the DARPin partially bound, receptor in a mixed state of partial activation — 11, a paradice: both bound and unbound effects simultaneously held

steps.domain_action: the unbound DARPin exists in solution, awaiting encounter with the NMDA receptor
the DARPin diffuses toward and engages the glycine-binding site on the NMDA receptor's GluN1 subunit
upon initial contact, the receptor's glycine site undergoes conformational selection — it branches into either an open (active) or closed (inactive) state
the receptor adopts the open conformation, allowing the DARPin to bind deeply and stabilize the active state
the DARPin's ankyrin repeats sequentially engage with the receptor surface, each repeat adding binding energy and specificity
the DARPin's own folded structure is recognized by the receptor as a stable, self-consistent binding partner
the sequential binding events reconstitute the receptor into a single, stabilized open conformation — the original therapeutic target is achieved
the DARPin's amino acid sequence is permanently recorded in the genetic code, ensuring the same binding event can be reproduced
the DARPin dissociates from the glycine site, returning the receptor to its dynamic equilibrium
if the receptor had adopted the closed conformation, the DARPin would fail to bind and the system would return to the unbound state without potentiation
in some cases, the DARPin may act as a partial agonist, simultaneously occupying the site and permitting only partial receptor activation — a paradice held without resolution
the entire system is bounded by the NMDA receptor's glycine-binding cleft, which contains and closes the therapeutic interaction

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the DARPin's ankyrin repeat library — the genetic and structural repertoire that translates the therapeutic intention into a specific binding protein
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: conformational coupling — the DARPin and receptor communicate through induced fit and conformational selection, a mechanical signal transduction
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the DARPin's folded structure — the tertiary fold stores the binding information and is retrieved upon each encounter with the receptor
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Brownian motion and diffusion — the random thermal motion of molecules determines the timing and order of binding events
phase_5.alfs_store: the PDB structure of the NMDA receptor (e.g., 4PE5) — the canonical reference that defines the glycine-binding site and guides DARPin design

phase_6.cycle_cost: free energy of binding (ΔG) — the thermodynamic cost of forming the DARPin-receptor complex
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: unbound DARPin and receptor in solution, with high configurational entropy
phase_6.post_cycle_state: bound complex with reduced configurational entropy but favorable enthalpy from hydrogen bonds and van der Waals interactions
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the binding cycle is entropy-conservative: the loss of translational and rotational entropy upon binding is compensated by the release of water molecules from the binding interface and the favorable enthalpy of the interaction
